validation_evidence.command: $path = '.specify/features/009-specify-discussions-workflows/implementation-matrix.md'; $text = Get-Content -Raw $path; $ids = @(); $ids += 1..30 | ForEach-Object { 'FR-{0:000}' -f $_ }; $ids += 1..6 | ForEach-Object { 'NFR-{0:000}' -f $_ }; $ids += 1..4 | ForEach-Object { 'SC-{0:000}' -f $_ }; $ids += 1..13 | ForEach-Object { 'MP-{0:000}' -f $_ }; $ids += 1..10 | ForEach-Object { 'CA-{0:000}' -f $_ }; $missing = $ids | Where-Object { $text -notmatch [regex]::Escape($_) }; [pscustomobject]@{ unmapped_literal = ($text -match 'unmapped_obligation_count`: `0`'); missing_count = $missing.Count; missing_ids = ($missing -join ', ') }

must_preserve_evidence.evidence: Implementation matrix keeps workflow phases mapped as execution contracts through T001, T002, T004, T005, T006, and T018; no requirement row reduces them to editor-only grouping.
The matrix covers the unified boundary across FR, NFR, SC, MP, and CA rows, with tasks spanning server, desktop, sharing, lifecycle, and verification surfaces.
FR-002, FR-017, FR-028, and T015/T016/T017 keep runtimeState session-owned and separate from template data.
FR-004 and FR-005 map to T002/T004/T015/T016 so guidance, policy, evidence, and gate strengths stay distinct.
FR-006 through FR-009 plus CA-001 and CA-002 map to T007/T008/T009/T010, preserving existing-skill bindings and advisory-only behavior.
FR-010 and FR-011 plus CA-006 map to T008/T009/T010/T015/T016, keeping soft audit bounded to used or clearly relevant skipped/unavailable skills.
FR-013 through FR-015 and T011/T012/T014 preserve template-plus-manifest sharing and exclude bundled skill contents.
FR-019 through FR-021 and T015/T016/T017 keep lifecycle state separate from completion submission outcome.
FR-022 through FR-024 and T015/T016/T017 preserve pending confirmation with Confirm/Reject/Retry and duplicate-ready blocking.
FR-025 through FR-027 and T015/T016/T017 preserve safe runtime controls and explicit authority labels.
FR-003, FR-028, SC-003, and T002/T004/T006/T011/T012 preserve compatibility-first adaptation before any destructive migration.
FR-005, FR-008, FR-013, FR-021, and T004/T008/T012/T016/T018 keep scheduler, auto-execution, bundles, and destructive migration out of scope.
T001, T006, and T018 keep live repository evidence authoritative instead of project cognition alone.

ca_evidence.evidence: T007, T008, T011, and T012 cover stable skill identity across local, bundled, plugin, managed, and MCP sources.
T007 and T008 keep priority semantics from overriding safety boundaries.
T007, T008, T011, T012, T013, and T014 cover visible degradation for missing, stale, disabled, invalid, ambiguous, plugin-disabled, unsupported-source, and unavailable skills.
T007 and T008 keep SkillTool permission handling explicit for skill-derived execution effects.
T008, T015, and T016 preserve skill provenance in running, pending, completed, resumed, stale-template, and missing-template session states.
T008, T009, T010, T015, and T016 keep used, skipped, and unavailable recommended skill decisions observable when relevant.
T002, T004, T011, and T012 validate workflow skill bindings across authoring, import/export, and session start paths.
T003, T005, T009, T010, T013, T014, and T017 distinguish recommended, unavailable, blocked, unable, pending, manual, auto, stale, and missing UI states.
T015, T016, and T018 preserve skill evidence, completion evidence, and transition provenance across compaction, resume, final reports, and imports.
T007 and T008 keep recursive or unbounded nested skill invocation prevented.
